cases.title: TS-26: Student already Present receives 409 DuplicateCheckIn on second check-in attempt
preconditions: Active session with valid room GPS and enrolled authenticated Student A
Student A attendance status is Present from prior successful check-in
Fresh Valid unexpired QrToken from GET /sessions/:sessionId/qr/current
Student A coordinates within session gpsRadiusMeters
steps: GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:tokenId/preflight using Student A session and assert HTTP 200 outcome Valid (BR-04 not evaluated at preflight)
POST /check-in with tokenId, in-radius coordinates, and spoofMetadata using Student A session
Assert HTTP status 409
Assert response outcome DuplicateCheckIn and errorCode DuplicateCheckIn
Assert message Bạn đã điểm danh buổi học này rồi per AC-09a
Query attendance_records for Student A and sessionId and assert exactly one Present row unchanged
Query check_in_attempts and assert latest attempt outcome DuplicateCheckIn
cases.expected: AC-09a satisfied: HTTP 409 DuplicateCheckIn semantics; no duplicate Present record per BR-04

cases.title: TS-27: Second student submitting Consumed token rejected with security log entry
preconditions: Active session with two enrolled students Student A and Student B
Student A successfully checked in; QrToken status Consumed by Student A
Token still within 30-second validity window (TestClock fixed before expiry)
Student B authenticated with in-radius coordinates
steps: GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:consumedTokenId/preflight as Student B and assert HTTP 403 errorCode TokenAlreadyUsed per API design §6.2
Assert message Mã QR đã được sử dụng per error-handling §4
Assert Student B attendance remains Pending (not Present)
Verify security log entry contains sessionId, tokenId, and submitting Student B id per AC-09b and BR-11
Optionally POST /check-in as Student B with same tokenId and assert HTTP 400 TokenAlreadyUsed as defense-in-depth per validation-rules §4 BR-11
cases.expected: AC-09b satisfied: consumed token reuse rejected at preflight (403) or submit (400); append-only security log with session ID, token ID, and submitting student ID

cases.title: TS-28: Parallel duplicate check-in requests from same student yield exactly one Present
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL test database running with serializable or row-lock isolation
Active session, enrolled student with Pending attendance, Valid QrToken
Two distinct Valid tokens available for parallel submission (or same token per race fixture)
DuplicateRace integration suite per testing-plan §5
steps: Fire two parallel POST /check-in or CheckInService.submit calls for same studentId and sessionId
Collect both HTTP responses or CheckInResult outcomes
Assert exactly one response is HTTP 200 outcome Success with attendance Present
Assert the other response is HTTP 409 outcome DuplicateCheckIn
Query attendance_records and assert exactly one Present row for studentId and sessionId
Query check_in_attempts and assert one Success and one DuplicateCheckIn outcome
cases.expected: AC-09c satisfied: concurrent duplicate requests produce exactly one Present; loser receives DuplicateCheckIn per NFR-02 integrity

cases.title: CheckInService.submit enforces one Present per student per session in PostgreSQL transaction
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with Active session, enrollment, Pending attendance_records row
Valid qr_tokens row with status Valid
CheckInService wired with AttendanceRecordRepository and QrTokenRepository per checkin-qr module §2.4
steps: Invoke CheckInService.submit with valid tokenId and in-radius locationPayload for studentId (first call)
Assert attendance transitions to Present and qr_token status Consumed
Invoke CheckInService.submit again for same studentId with new Valid tokenId
Assert second call throws or returns DuplicateCheckIn without creating second Present row
Verify unique constraint on (session_id, student_id) per domain model §4.2
cases.expected: Module boundary enforces BR-04: at most one automated Present per student per session; duplicate rejected at CheckInService layer

cases.title: DuplicateRace: 20 concurrent CheckInService.submit calls for same student produce one Present
preconditions: DuplicateRace integration suite per testing-plan §5
Active session with enrolled student Pending attendance
20 parallel workers each holding valid tokenId (unique tokens or controlled race fixture)
TestClock within attendance window and token TTL
steps: Launch 20 parallel CheckInService.submit invocations for identical studentId and sessionId
Await all completions
Count Success vs DuplicateCheckIn outcomes across results
Assert exactly 1 Success and 19 DuplicateCheckIn (or 1 Success and remainder rejected)
Query attendance_records and assert single Present row with one checkedInAt timestamp
cases.expected: AC-09c concurrency drill passes: row-level lock on attendance prevents double Present under parallel load per error-handling §9 Parallel race drill

cases.title: POST /check-in returns 409 DuplicateCheckIn envelope when student already Present
preconditions: Authenticated enrolled Student session with Present attendance for target session
Valid unexpired QrToken for Active session
steps: Send POST /api/v1/check-in with body { tokenId, latitude: 10.762700, longitude: 106.660200, spoofMetadata: { mockLocationDetected: false } }
Assert HTTP status 409 Conflict
Assert Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8
Assert response body outcome DuplicateCheckIn and errorCode DuplicateCheckIn
Assert response body message Bạn đã điểm danh buổi học này rồi
Assert no attendance field with updated Present on failure envelope per API design §6.2
cases.expected: HTTP 409 DuplicateCheckIn contract per API design §6.3 and §1 idempotency note; canonical failure envelope for duplicate student

cases.title: POST /check-in returns 400 TokenAlreadyUsed envelope for Consumed token submission
preconditions: QrToken row with status Consumed linked to Active session
Authenticated enrolled Student B who did not consume the token
Token issuedAt within 30-second window (not ExpiredQr path)
steps: POST /api/v1/check-in with consumed tokenId and in-radius coordinates using Student B session
Assert HTTP status 400 Bad Request
Assert response body outcome TokenAlreadyUsed and errorCode TokenAlreadyUsed
Assert response body message Mã QR đã được sử dụng per validation-rules §4 BR-11 mapping
Verify CheckInAttempt row inserted with outcome TokenAlreadyUsed per FR-09
cases.expected: HTTP 400 TokenAlreadyUsed contract for one-time-use token violation at submit layer; distinct from 409 DuplicateCheckIn per BR-11

cases.title: Flow B steps 3–4: Student A duplicate retry then Student B same-token reuse in anti-fraud path
preconditions: Flow B steps 1–2 complete: Active session with valid QR, Student A checked in successfully
Student A attendance Present; tokenId from step 2 status Consumed
Student B enrolled and authenticated with in-radius coordinates
steps: Student A GET preflight with fresh Valid tokenId and assert HTTP 200 outcome Valid
Student A POST /check-in again with fresh Valid tokenId and assert HTTP 409 DuplicateCheckIn
Student B GET preflight with same tokenId consumed in step 2 and assert HTTP 403 TokenAlreadyUsed
Verify security log entry for Student B attempt with sessionId, tokenId, studentId
Assert Student B attendance remains Pending
Confirm Flow B anti-fraud rejection path per testing-plan §6 Flow B tags AC-09, BR-04, BR-11
cases.expected: Flow B validates duplicate student and token-sharing rejection in sequence; AC-09a and AC-09b covered in anti-fraud e2e flow

cases.title: Duplicate check-in leaves attendance afterState Present unchanged (no second transition)
preconditions: attendance_records row with beforeState Present and non-null checkedInAt
Active session and Valid QrToken for duplicate attempt
CheckInService.submit callable for same studentId
steps: Record beforeState: status Present, checkedInAt T0
Invoke CheckInService.submit with new valid tokenId
Assert result outcome DuplicateCheckIn (not Success)
Query attendance_records and assert afterState status still Present
Assert checkedInAt unchanged at T0 (no overwrite)
Verify SM-04 outcome DuplicateCheckIn maps to no attendance state change per 07-state-machines.md §5
cases.expected: beforeState Present; afterState Present unchanged; DuplicateCheckIn is terminal rejection without duplicate Present record

cases.title: Instructor denied POST /check-in preventing proxy check-in via staff account
preconditions: Authenticated Instructor session available
Active session with Valid QrToken and in-radius coordinates
Instructor role lacks checkin:submit per roles-permissions §2.2
steps: POST /check-in with valid tokenId and coordinates using Instructor session cookie
Assert HTTP status 403 Forbidden and errorCode Forbidden
Verify no Present attendance created for instructor userId
Assert permission guard runs at pipeline step 3 before BR-04 duplicate evaluation
cases.expected: Only Student role holds checkin:submit; staff accounts cannot bypass one-student-per-account rule via instructor login per anti-proxy identity binding BR-04 scope

cases.title: Duplicate check-in by Present student does not consume submitted token for other students
preconditions: Student A already Present for session
Fresh Valid QrToken T1 not yet consumed
Student B enrolled with Pending attendance awaiting check-in
steps: Student A POST /check-in with tokenId T1 and assert HTTP 409 DuplicateCheckIn
Query qr_tokens for T1 and assert status remains Valid (not Consumed)
Student B GET preflight with tokenId T1 and assert HTTP 200 outcome Valid
Student B POST /check-in with same tokenId T1 and in-radius coordinates
Assert Student B HTTP 200 outcome Success and attendance Present
Query qr_tokens for T1 and assert status Consumed after Student B success
cases.expected: DuplicateCheckIn failure does not consume token per domain model §5.2; token remains available for legitimate second student until successful consumption

cases.title: Mobile check-in shows DuplicateCheckIn message with prior check-in time and history link
preconditions: Authenticated Student on mobile viewport 375×812 with Present attendance for session
Active session with valid check-in deep link /check-in?token=:tokenId&session=:sessionId
GPS permission granted with in-radius coordinates
steps: Navigate to /check-in deep link for session where student is already Present
Assert validating_token inline spinner during GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:tokenId/preflight
Assert GpsCaptureStep mounts after preflight returns HTTP 200 outcome Valid per BR-15
Wait for GpsCaptureStep ready substate (Vị trí đã sẵn sàng; no spinner; aria-busy false; submit enabled)
Tap Xác nhận điểm danh submit control
Verify CheckInOutcomePanel displays info-level DuplicateCheckIn state
Verify message Bạn đã điểm danh buổi học này rồi per user-flows §WF-03
Verify prior check-in timestamp displayed
Verify Xem lịch sử link navigates to /history per ui-states DuplicateCheckIn action
cases.expected: AC-09a surfaced in mobile web UI after preflight pass and GPS ready; student informed of prior check-in with history navigation per CheckInOutcomePanel traceability

cases.title: Mobile check-in shows token reuse rejection when scanning shared Consumed QR
preconditions: Student B authenticated on mobile viewport
QrToken already Consumed by Student A within current 30-second window
Deep link or scanned QR contains consumed tokenId for Active session
steps: Student B opens /check-in?token=:consumedTokenId&session=:sessionId
Assert validating_token inline spinner during GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:consumedTokenId/preflight
Verify preflight returns 403 with errorCode TokenAlreadyUsed and message Mã QR đã được sử dụng
Assert data-testid gps-capture-step is absent; GpsCaptureStep never mounts per BR-15
Verify error outcome displayed on scan step with TokenAlreadyUsed state
Verify no success confirmation or Present badge shown
Verify recovery guidance to scan fresh QR from projector
cases.expected: AC-09b anti-proxy token sharing rejection visible at preflight on mobile web UI; GPS step unreachable; student directed to obtain new rotating QR per BR-11

cases.title: CheckInTransaction atomically consumes QrToken only on Success not on TokenAlreadyUsed
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with qr_tokens row status Consumed
Enrolled Student B with Pending attendance
CheckInTransaction suite per testing-plan §5 CheckInTransaction target
steps: Begin transaction context and invoke CheckInService.submit with Consumed tokenId for Student B
Assert outcome TokenAlreadyUsed and transaction rolled back without Present update
Assert qr_tokens row remains Consumed (no double-consumption attempt persisted)
Contrast: successful submit on Valid token atomically sets Consumed and Present in single commit
cases.expected: CheckInTransaction NFR-02 target: Present + token consumed atomically on Success only; token reuse rejected without corrupting attendance state

cases.title: Sequential duplicate submissions: first 200 Success then second 409 without duplicate Present rows
preconditions: Authenticated enrolled Student with Pending attendance
Two sequential Valid QrTokens T1 and T2 for Active session
In-radius coordinates and TestClock within window
steps: POST /check-in with tokenId T1 and assert HTTP 200 outcome Success
Immediately POST /check-in with tokenId T2 for same student
Assert HTTP 409 outcome DuplicateCheckIn on second request
Query attendance_records and assert count of Present rows for studentId and sessionId equals 1
Verify error-handling §9 Duplicate check-in drill expected outcome
cases.expected: Sequential boundary: first submission wins Present; immediate retry returns 409 DuplicateCheckIn per local development failure drill

cases.title: Preflight rejects consumed token with TokenAlreadyUsed and security log before GPS step
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with Active session and qr_tokens row status Consumed by Student A
Token still within 30-second validity window
Authenticated enrolled Student B session with Pending attendance
steps: Invoke GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:consumedTokenId/preflight with Student B session
Assert HTTP status 403 Forbidden and errorCode TokenAlreadyUsed
Assert message Mã QR đã được sử dụng per API design §6.2
Verify security log TokenReuseAlert entry contains sessionId, tokenId, and Student B id
Query attendance_records and qr_tokens and assert no writes occurred (read-only preflight per validation-rules §3.6a)
cases.expected: AC-09b satisfied at preflight layer: consumed token reuse rejected with 403 TokenAlreadyUsed and security log; GPS capture unreachable per BR-15

cases.title: Preflight returns 200 for already-Present student; DuplicateCheckIn rejected only at POST
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with Active session, enrolled Student A with attendance status Present
Fresh Valid unexpired QrToken not yet consumed
Authenticated Student A session with checkin:submit permission
steps: Invoke GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:tokenId/preflight with Student A session
Assert HTTP status 200 and outcome Valid with session summary (classCode, subjectCode, roomName)
Query attendance_records and qr_tokens and assert no writes from preflight alone
Invoke POST /api/v1/check-in with same tokenId and in-radius coordinates
Assert HTTP status 409 outcome DuplicateCheckIn per BR-04 evaluated at submit layer only
cases.expected: BR-04 duplicate guard runs at POST not preflight; already-Present student passes preflight enabling GPS step but receives DuplicateCheckIn on submit per validation-rules §3.6a vs §4 BR-04
